context: Now put your stories somewhere. A good trip story names the places you went and says what the weather did. These are your places - the Albir lighthouse, the mountain, the beach - not a list of museums you never visit.
explanation: Two anchors first: el faro = the lighthouse (the flat seaside walk to the Faro de l'Albir) and la montaña = the mountain (the climb up behind Albir). Point your past verbs at them: caminé al faro, subí la montaña. The weather words - what it did that day - come next.

examples.es: caminé al faro y hice fotos
examples.en: I walked to the lighthouse and took photos
examples.note: el faro = the lighthouse; las fotos = the photos.

examples.en: I climbed the mountain in Albir

examples.es: fui a la playa y hizo sol
examples.en: I went to the beach and it was sunny
examples.note: hizo sol = it was sunny (the weather "did sun").
hizo calor = it was hot; compare tengo calor = I am hot.

examples.es: el tiempo fue malo, llovió mucho
examples.en: the weather was bad, it rained a lot
examples.note: el tiempo = the weather; llovió = it rained (past of llueve).
